urgency.message: 1 critical CVE(s) and 6 new KEV addition(s) in the last 7 days. Review immediately.

items.title: Name That Toon Contest
Inspector general finds NIST mistakes have made vulnerability database ineffective
items.summary: NIST’s National Vulnerability Database (NVD) backlog mushroomed from 13,000 unprocessed security vulnerabilities in February 2024 to more than 27,000 by the end of 2025, “undermining the NVD’s utility and public trust," according to an inspector general report.

items.title: NSA selects new leads for key cybersecurity posts
items.summary: David Imbordino, an NSA senior executive who most recently led its cybersecurity directorate in an acting capacity, has been named as its new chief. Bruce Jones, a career NSA technical and operational leader, as the new head of its Cybersecurity Collaboration Center.

items.title: WP Maps Pro Vulnerability Exploited to Take Over WordPress Sites
items.summary:
The security defect (CVE-2026-8732) allows unauthenticated attackers to create administrative accounts on the affected installations.
The post WP Maps Pro Vulnerability Exploited to Take Over WordPress Sites appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Dashlane password manager users locked out by brute force attacks
items.summary: Multiple Dashlane users have been locked out of their accounts following brute-force attacks that attempted logins from distant locations and unknown devices. [...]

items.title: Dutch Police Dismantle Massive 17-Million-Device Botnet
items.summary:
Dutch authorities seized command-and-control servers tied to a botnet of infected computers, smartphones, and tablets that was allegedly used to power a residential proxy network and facilitate cybercrime.
The post Dutch Police Dismantle Massive 17-Million-Device Botnet appeared first on SecurityWee

items.title: Miasma Supply Chain Attack Compromises Red Hat npm Packages with Credential-Stealing Worm
items.summary:
A new Mini Shai-Hulud supply chain attack campaign, codenamed Miasma, has compromised @redhat-cloud-services packages to steal credentials and secrets from developer machines and deliver a self-propagating worm.

"This is effectively a Mini Shai-Hulud campaign: it uses the same core tactics of insta

items.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
items.summary: The Instagram accounts for the Obama White House and the Chief Master Sergeant of the U.S. Space Force were briefly defaced with pro-Iranian images and messages over the weekend, after instructions began circulating on Telegram showing how to trick Meta's "AI support assistant" bot into resetting ac

items.title: WordPress malware campaign hides payloads in Steam profiles
items.summary: Nearly 2,000 WordPress websites were infected with malware that relies on Steam Community profile comments to hide command-and-control (C2) data. [...]

items.title: Critical Windows Netlogon Vulnerability in Attackers’ Crosshairs
items.summary:
Organizations are advised to patch CVE-2026-41089 as soon as possible, given its severity, the potential ongoing exploitation.
The post Critical Windows Netlogon Vulnerability in Attackers’ Crosshairs appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.summary: For the latest discoveries in cyber research for the week of 1st June, please download our Threat Intelligence Bulletin. TOP ATTACKS AND BREACHES Carnival Corporation, a global cruise line operator, has confirmed a data breach affecting nearly 6 million people after attackers used social engineering

items.title: Microsoft investigates Office Apps, Teams file access issues
items.summary: Microsoft says an ongoing incident is preventing users of its Teams collaboration platform and Office for the web cloud-based productivity suite from opening files. [...]

items.title: Patch Now: Another Palo Alto Auth Bypass Bug Under Active Exploit
items.summary: Exploiting the PAN-OS GlobalProtect VPN vulnerability requires certain conditions, but adversaries have done so in two attack waves that started in mid-May.

items.title: Race Against Time: Why Faster Vulnerability Alerts Matter
items.summary: Attackers are exploiting vulnerabilities faster than many organizations can identify and patch them. SecAlerts explains why faster vulnerability alerts can help reduce exposure and improve response times. [...]

items.summary: Added Visual Studio software to the Security Updates table.  Customers that are running supported version of Visual Studio are encouraged to update to the indicated version to be protected from this vulnerability.

items.title: Critical Flowise Flaw Gives Attackers Full Server Control
items.summary: Obsidian publishes PoC for a 1-click Flowise RCE that can fully compromise self-hosted servers

items.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: New Linux Flaw, PAN-OS Exploit, AI-Powered Attacks, OAuth Phishing and More
items.summary:
Monday hit like a cron job with anger issues.

A busted auth path here, a repo-side faceplant there, some "patched-ish" thing already getting chewed on in the wild, and then the usual bonus round: poisoned dev tools, sketchy forum chatter, phishing kits pretending to be productivity, and AI lowering

items.title: Infosecurity Europe: Tabletop Exercise to Test How CISOs Respond to Major Supermarket Cyber-Attack
items.summary: Semperis is set to bring ‘Enter the War Room: A Tabletop Experience’ to Infosecurity Europe to help cybersecurity leaders prepare to face real incidents

items.title: Dragos Acquires xIoT Security Firm Phosphorus
items.summary:
Dragos said customers will soon gain expanded asset visibility and integrated device intelligence, with automated remediation workflows and a unified platform experience to follow.
The post Dragos Acquires xIoT Security Firm Phosphorus appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Critical Windows Netlogon RCE flaw now exploited in attacks
items.summary: The Centre for Cybersecurity Belgium (CCB), the country's national authority for cybersecurity, warned on Friday that threat actors are now exploiting a recently patched critical Windows Netlogon vulnerability in attacks. [...]

items.title: Microsoft says it will not pursue security researchers after zero-day backlash
items.summary: Microsoft said it is taking the feedback seriously, adding: “To be clear about our approach to legal matters, we have no intention to pursue action against individuals conducting or publishing their security research.”

items.title: Webinar tomorrow: From alert to resolution in network incident response
items.summary: Network incidents are often detected quickly, but investigations and coordination can delay resolution. Join our webinar tomorrow to learn how automation and AI-assisted workflows can help IT teams accelerate incident response. [...]

items.title: China-Aligned Groups Ramp Up Attacks: Dragon Weave Hits Czech Republic & Taiwan
items.summary:
A new cyber espionage campaign codenamed Operation Dragon Weave has been observed targeting officials and citizens in the Czech Republic and Taiwan to deliver an AdaptixC2 agent.

According to Seqrite Labs, targets of the campaign include government, research, academic, technology, and financial ser

items.title: As the Pentagon Pushes for Battlefield AI, Some Military Leaders Urge Caution
items.summary:
AI’s use in the military is part of the administration’s larger push to grow the capability it sees as a unique American advantage.
The post As the Pentagon Pushes for Battlefield AI, Some Military Leaders Urge Caution appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Microsoft fixes outage affecting MFA setup, MySignIn service
items.summary: Microsoft is working to address an ongoing incident preventing customers from setting up multi-factor authentication (MFA) or accessing the My Sign-Ins platform. [...]

items.title: The Security Growth Platform: Why MSPs Are Moving Beyond vCISO Tools
items.summary:
Three years ago, the practical question for an MSP building a cybersecurity practice was which "vCISO platform" to buy. The term was good shorthand for the work at the time: assessments, advisory, reporting, maybe a compliance module bolted on the side. The work has since outgrown the descriptor.

items.title: 19-Year-Old Linux Kernel Vulnerability Exposes Systems to Root Access
items.summary:
Proof-of-concept (PoC) exploit code has been released for the CIFSwitch flaw, which allows low-privileged users to escalate to root on vulnerable Linux systems.
The post 19-Year-Old Linux Kernel Vulnerability Exposes Systems to Root Access appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: FSB Group Gamaredon Hides Worm in Windows Data Streams
items.summary: FSB-linked Gamaredon concealed a fileless worm in NTFS data streams to spy on Ukraine targets

items.title: Microsoft fixes KB5089549 Windows security update install issues
items.summary: Microsoft has resolved a known issue causing installation failures and 0x800f0922 errors when deploying the May 2026 Windows 11 security update (KB5089549). [...]

items.summary: In this excerpt from WIRED Book Club pick The Yahoo Boys, journalist Carlos Barragán traces one scammer’s journey from flop to fortune.

items.title: Recent Palo Alto Networks Vulnerability Exploited for Weeks

Hackers began exploiting CVE-2026-0257, an authentication bypass in Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS, four days after public disclosure.
The post Recent Palo Alto Networks Vulnerability Exploited for Weeks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.summary:

The tool, named codexui-android, is advertised on GitHub and npm as a remote web UI for OpenAI Codex, attracting over

items.title: Websites Can Now Spy on You Through Your Hard Drive
items.summary: Thanks to the newly detailed FROST technique, telltale SSD activity can be measured in the browser using simple JavaScript.

items.title: Critical WP Maps Pro Flaw Actively Exploited to Create Admin Accounts
items.summary:
Threat actors are attempting to actively exploit a critical security flaw impacting WP Maps Pro, a WordPress plugin that has had over 15,000 sales on the Envato Market, to create malicious administrator accounts on susceptible sites.

WP Maps Pro allows site owners to embed customizable Google Maps

items.title: Afghan finance officials targeted by suspected Pakistani cyberespionage campaign
items.summary: A suspected Pakistan-linked hacking group has targeted Afghanistan's Ministry of Finance and provincial government officials in a new cyberespionage campaign, researchers have found.

items.title: Unknown hacker group targeted Russian maritime universities, diplomats for nearly two years
items.summary: More than half of the attacks observed over the past year targeted educational institutions, particularly maritime universities and schools that train personnel for Russia's shipping, inland waterway and fishing industries.

items.summary:
Dutch authorities have announced the takedown of a botnet that enslaved millions of infected devices, including computers, tablets, smartphones, and IoT devices, to carry out malicious attacks.
